gaps: Some broadcast records use adopted_items rather than consensus_item_id
Bundle broadcast fields are inconsistent or missing
key_points: Protocol defines broadcasts ledger and launcher exports STAMPEDE_COLLAB_BROADCASTS; monitor displays broadcast count.
Commander prompt asks commanders to consume and append broadcasts.
Broadcast consumption is prompt-only; merger/synthesis code does not reference broadcasts/collab ledgers.

gaps: Bundle schema in commander agent omits fields launcher requires
key_points: Consensus items generally include source_refs and often link back to improvements/proposals/reviews.
One consensus item links to squad atom files rather than improvement ledger items; one improvement references consensus, creating chain-order drift.
Bundles adopt consensus inconsistently: adopted_consensus_item_ids vs adopted_consensus, and merger does not consume collab ledgers.
protocol.json defines required_fields: ts, run_id, commander_id, event, item_id, summary, evidence, confidence, source_refs.
Launcher creates ledgers and prompts commanders to write required fields, but monitor only counts lines and no validator enforces schemas.
Current run records show schema drift: some reviews/broadcasts miss evidence, evidence types vary, and adoption fields differ across bundles/results.

gaps: Line-count observability can be wrong for partial lines/missing trailing newline
key_points: Protocol guidance explicitly says readers must tolerate absent files and malformed trailing lines.
Collab ledgers are pre-created; monitor uses existence checks and wc -l, so malformed JSON does not crash monitor counts.
Tolerance is implicit because most paths do not parse JSONL; partial/malformed lines can still distort counts.

gaps: No atomic updater for state.json counts
No schema/invariant validation for swarm-state telemetry
atoms_received semantics ambiguous per squad vs per worker
key_points: state.json task and collab counts are stale/frozen while filesystem and ledgers show real progress.
state.json collab.counts reports all zeros while observed ledgers contain proposals=5, reviews=11, improvements=7, consensus=5, broadcasts≈4.

Commander telemetry varies: commander-002 had truncation/recovery indicators, commander-004 has impossible completed>launched counts, commander-005 overclaims worker tracking relative to child-agents evidence.

overall_gaps_ranked.gap: state.json and some swarm-state telemetry are stale or internally inconsistent, so monitor/proof counts can be misleading.
Collaboration bus schemas are defined but not validated, leading to missing required fields and field-name/type drift.
Consensus/adoption/broadcast outputs are not consumed by merger/synthesis, so cross-commander consensus remains prompt-level only.
Malformed-line tolerance relies on not parsing ledgers, which avoids crashes but can hide corruption and inaccurate counts.

recommendations_review_only: Add a tolerant JSONL reader/validator that reports malformed lines and missing required fields without rewriting ledgers.
Make state/count derivation filesystem-ledger based or update state.json atomically from append events.
Align commander agent bundle schema with launcher prompt: adopted_consensus_item_ids, source_refs, broadcasts.
Teach merger/synthesis to consume collab ledgers and adopted consensus items.
summary: Collaboration-bus QA found protocol intent present but enforcement and telemetry are inconsistent: required fields are prompt-guided not validated, state counts are stale, malformed-line tolerance is mostly non-parsing/line-count tolerance, consensus linkage is partially valid with bundle schema drift, and broadcasts are visible but not consumed by merger/synthesis.
